content:
# Judges Chapter 7 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), c. 1008 CE | **Source Image:** source-texts/hebrew-OT/JDG_page_275.jpg, JDG_page_276.jpg

1. And Yerubba'al — that is, Gid'on — and all the people who [were] with him rose early and encamped beside the spring of Charod. And the camp of Midyan was north of him, from the hill of Moreh, in the valley.

2. And YHWH said to Gid'on, "The people who [are] with you [are] too many for me to give Midyan into their hand, lest Yisrael glorify itself against me, saying, 'My own hand has saved me.'

3. And now, proclaim in the ears of the people, saying, 'Whoever [is] fearful and trembling, let him return and depart from Mount Gil'ad.'" And twenty-two thousand of the people returned, and ten thousand remained.

4. And YHWH said to Gid'on, "The people [are] still too many. Bring them down to the water, and I will test them for you there. And it shall be, of whom I say to you, 'This one shall go with you,' he shall go with you; and of whom I say to you, 'This one shall not go with you,' he shall not go."

5. And he brought the people down to the water. And YHWH said to Gid'on, "Everyone who laps from the water with his tongue, as a dog laps, you shall set him apart; and everyone who kneels on his knees to drink."

6. And the number of those who lapped, putting their hand to their mouth, was three hundred men. And all the rest of the people knelt on their knees to drink water.

7. And YHWH said to Gid'on, "By the three hundred men who lapped I will save you, and I will give Midyan into your hand. And let all the [other] people go, each to his place."

8. And the people took provisions in their hand, and their shofars. And all the men of Yisrael he sent away, each to his tent, but the three hundred men he retained. And the camp of Midyan was below him in the valley.

9. And it was that night, YHWH said to him, "Arise, go down against the camp, for I have given it into your hand.

10. And if you are afraid to go down, go down — you and Purah your servant — to the camp.

11. And you shall hear what they speak, and afterward your hands shall be strengthened, and you shall go down against the camp." And he went down — he and Purah his servant — to the edge of the armed men who [were] in the camp.

12. And Midyan and Amaleq and all the sons of the east were lying in the valley like locusts for multitude, and their camels were without number, like the sand that [is] on the shore of the sea for multitude.

13. And Gid'on came, and behold, a man was recounting a dream to his companion. And he said, "Behold, a dream I dreamed, and behold, a cake of barley bread was tumbling into the camp of Midyan. And it came to the tent and struck it, and it fell, and it overturned it upward, and the tent fell."

14. And his companion answered and said, "This [is] nothing other than the sword of Gid'on son of Yo'ash, a man of Yisrael. God has given into his hand Midyan and all the camp."

15. And it was, when Gid'on heard the telling of the dream and its interpretation, he bowed down. And he returned to the camp of Yisrael and said, "Arise, for YHWH has given the camp of Midyan into your hand."

16. And he divided the three hundred men [into] three companies. And he put shofars in the hand of all of them and empty jars and torches inside the jars.

17. And he said to them, "Look at me and do likewise. And behold, when I come to the edge of the camp, it shall be — as I do, so shall you do.

18. And I will blow the shofar — I and all who [are] with me — and you shall blow the shofars, also you, all around the camp, and you shall say, 'For YHWH and for Gid'on!'"

19. And Gid'on and the hundred men who [were] with him came to the edge of the camp at the beginning of the middle watch — they had just set the watch — and they blew the shofars and shattered the jars that [were] in their hand.

20. And the three companies blew the shofars and broke the jars. And they held in their left hand the torches and in their right hand the shofars to blow. And they called out, "A sword for YHWH and for Gid'on!"

21. And each man stood in his place all around the camp. And all the camp ran, and they shouted, and they fled.

22. And the three hundred blew the shofars, and YHWH set the sword of each man against his companion and against all the camp. And the camp fled as far as Beit-Shittah toward Tsererah, as far as the border of Avel-M'cholah, by Tabbath.

23. And the men of Yisrael were called together from Naphtali and from Asher and from all M'nashsheh, and they pursued after Midyan.

24. And Gid'on sent messengers through all the hill-country of Ephrayim, saying, "Come down to meet Midyan, and seize the waters against them as far as Beit-Barah and the Yarden." And every man of Ephrayim was called together, and they seized the waters as far as Beit-Barah and the Yarden.

25. And they captured the two princes of Midyan — Orev and Ze'ev. And they killed Orev at the rock of Orev, and Ze'ev they killed at the winepress of Ze'ev. And they pursued Midyan, and the heads of Orev and Ze'ev they brought to Gid'on across the Yarden.

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **v.3 — "Mount Gil'ad":** The MT reads har haGil'ad ("Mount Gilead"), but Gilead is east of the Jordan, far from the scene of action in the Jezreel Valley. Many scholars emend to har haGilboa' ("Mount Gilboa"), which is geographically sensible. EMET preserves the MT reading.

2. **v.13 — "a cake of barley bread":** ts'lil lechem s'orim — literally "a round/tumbling [thing] of barley bread." Barley bread was the food of the poor. The dream symbolizes Gideon: a humble, common thing that overturns the mighty tent of Midian.

3. **v.20 — "A sword for YHWH and for Gid'on":** cherev laYHWH ul'Gid'on — the battle cry shifts from v.18's "for YHWH and for Gid'on" to include "a sword." The three hundred had no swords — only shofars, jars, and torches. The "sword" is YHWH's, not theirs.

— EMET, AI Translator
